rulings.Case Title: JOHN JIMENEZ VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 10, 2009, line 1, DEFENDANT FOSTER WHEELER LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion denied. Triable issue of fact raised whether plaintiff was exposed to defendant's original asbestos material at the Harvey aluminum worksite. (Plaintiff's deposition and declaration.) Objections to Ay declaration overruled. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM COOPER et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 10, 2009, line 5, DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for summary judgment denied. Motion for summary adjudication granted in part and denied in part. Motion denied as to government contractor defense. Triable issues of fact raised whether Navy approved reasonably precise specifications. No evidence that the government exercised its discretion over the design defect, the inclusion of asbestos. Summary adjudication granted as to false representation, loss of consortium, and premises/owner liability as unopposed. Summary adjudication granted as to punitive damages. Defendant shifted the burden and plaintiff failed to show that defendant acted with malice, oppression, or fraud. The responses to special interrogatories only contain conclusory allegations that defendant was generally aware of the dangers of asbestos, which is not sufficient. Plaintiff's separate statement of fact number eleven conclusory. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND R. HUFF VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings On Pltf'S First Amended Complaint For Damages (Libel And Slander); Memorandum Of P/A;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 10, 2009, line 10, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO Motion For Judgment on the Pleadings on Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint for Damages (Libel And Slander). Granted, without leave to amend. No opposition filed. Paragraph 18 of the Amended Complaint and attached exhibits are insufficient as a matter of law to plead compliance with the Government Claims Act. =(302/CWW)
